name: Pantano de Pena Loop
name_original: Vuelta al Pantano de Pena — circular desde Valderrobres
description: This moderate 18.9-mile mountain biking route circles the scenic Pena reservoir near Valderrobres, taking about 2 hours and 45 minutes.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Valderrobres offers diverse landscapes, from ancient olive and almond groves to crystal-clear mountain streams. The region is situated on the edge of Els Ports Natural Park, providing a backdrop of mountains and varied ecosystems. Riders can explore extensive networks of trails that wind through lush vineyards and along the Matarraña river. The area's natural features, including the Pena Reservoir and El Parrizal, create a unique environment for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Valderrobres The most popular mountain bike trail is Pantano de Pena Loop, an 18.9 miles (30.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers picturesque views around the Pena Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is From Matarraña to the World Trail, a difficult 14.5 miles (23.3 km) path. This route incorporates the Vía Verde and Matarraña Natural Path, providing varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Pantano de Pena – Pena Reservoir loop from Valderrobres / Vall de Roures, a 16.4 miles (26.4 km) trail leading through scenic views of the Pena Reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Mountain biking around Valderrobres is defined by the rugged terrain of Els Ports Natural Park, the serene Pena Reservoir, and ancient olive groves. The network offers options for all skill levels, from family-friendly excursions to challenging routes. The routes in Valderrobres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 250 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Valderrobres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Valderrobres?
A: Valderrobres offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 30 routes available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 2 easy, 21 moderate, and 8 difficult options, ensuring there's something for every rider.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Valderrobres?
A: The climate in Valderrobres is generally favorable for mountain biking year-round. Summers are warm but more bearable than coastal areas due to the town's 500-meter altitude and lower humidity. Spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures for exploring the trails.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Valderrobres?
A: Yes, Valderrobres has routes suitable for beginners and families. While many trails are moderate, there are 2 easy routes available. For a family-friendly option, consider the 'Del Matarraña al Mundo' route, which incorporates the Vía Verde and Matarraña Natural Path, offering a basic and enjoyable experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails in Valderrobres?
A: The terrain around Valderrobres is incredibly diverse. You can expect to ride through ancient olive and almond groves, lush vineyards, and along crystal-clear mountain streams. Many routes traverse the rugged landscapes of Els Ports Natural Park, offering varied surfaces from smooth paths to more challenging rocky sections.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Valderrobres?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular feature in Valderrobres. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10418848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valderrobres Stone Bridge – View of La Portellada loop from Valderrobres / Vall de Roures&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 38.6 km trail that offers scenic views and a complete loop back to your starting point.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails in Valderrobres?
A: The trails around Valderrobres are rich with natural beauty. You can encounter stunning features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3425404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; with its striking water colors, the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4978626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parrissal Walkways&lt;/a&gt; where the Matarraña river carves through rocks, and the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer views of the majestic Els Ports Natural Park.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by mountain bike in the Valderrobres area?
A: Yes, the region boasts beautiful waterfalls. The 'Un Salto al Tastavins' route, for example, features a 25-meter waterfall and offers views of the Puertos de Beceite. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a 30-meter cascade formed by the Sénia River, is another stunning natural highlight in the vicinity.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter while mountain biking in Valderrobres?
A: While exploring the trails, you might spot a variety of wildlife. The region is home to vultures, ibex, foxes, and even wild boar. The diverse ecosystems of Els Ports Natural Park and the surrounding areas provide a rich habitat for these animals.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Valderrobres?
A: The mountain biking experience in Valderrobres is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained trails, and the stunning natural features like the Pena Reservoir and the rugged terrain of Els Ports Natural Park.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes around Valderrobres?
A: For those seeking longer rides, Valderrobres offers several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17510195" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverside of Valderrobres – Pena Reservoir loop from Valderrobres / Vall de Roures&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 48 km route that provides an extensive exploration of the area's natural beauty and takes approximately 4 hours to complete.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Valderrobres?
A: Valderrobres is well-equipped for visitors, and parking is generally available in and around the town, often near the starting points of popular routes. It's advisable to check specific trailhead information for the most convenient parking options for your chosen route.

Q: Can I reach Valderrobres by public transport with my bike?
A: While Valderrobres is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services like Hife, transporting bikes can sometimes be subject to specific operator policies and space availability. It's recommended to check with the bus company directly regarding their bike carriage regulations before planning your journey.
tips: We are in Valderrobres, known for being "One of the most beautiful towns in Spain" where its stone bridge and the Plaza Mayor with the town hall, the castle and/or the church stand out.

We can find more information at the tourist office or the website https://www.beceite.es/
The Pena reservoir is located within the Matarraña region surrounded by beautiful mountains such as the Sierra de Arcas that make this place a must for nature and sports lovers.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Vuelta al Pantano de Pena mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered moderate for mountain biking, suitable for riders with good physical condition. While much of it is accessible, some sections, particularly around the reservoir, can be more demanding and might require advanced skills or even dismounting and pushing your bike. There is a specific 4.53 km section from the dam to the Pena river that is of low difficulty, which could be a good option for a shorter, easier ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail predominantly features mostly paved tracks, including a 3.5 km rural asphalt road known as Camino dels Olivars, which starts from the Santa Ana hermitage. As you circle the reservoir, some portions might transition to forest tracks. Be prepared for varied surfaces, and remember that some sections could be challenging enough to require pushing your bike.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vuelta al Pantano de Pena trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Valderrobres, making it convenient to find parking within the town. Specific parking areas for trail access are usually well-marked, especially near the starting point of the circular route.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy picturesque views as you cycle around the Pantano de Pena, which is nestled at the foot of the Ports of Beceite. The route also passes by the Riverside of Valderrobres and the charming settlement of Beceite. The reservoir itself is an older structure, built in 1930, offering a tranquil setting.

faqs.question: Is the Vuelta al Pantano de Pena trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife, other trail users, or near roads. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the natural beauty of the area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike the Vuelta al Pantano de Pena is typically during the spring and autumn months when the weather is milder. Summers can be quite warm, though swimming is possible in certain spots of the reservoir. Winters are generally cooler but can still be suitable depending on conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: Since the route starts and ends in Valderrobres, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and other amenities in the town before or after your ride. Along the trail itself, facilities are limited, so it's best to carry enough water and snacks.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to mountain bike the Vuelta al Pantano de Pena trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Can I rent equipment like pedal boats or kayaks at the reservoir?
faqs.answer: Yes, during the warmer summer months, visitors can rent pedal boats or kayaks at the Pantano de Pena, adding another recreational dimension to your visit beyond mountain biking.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that are part of official trails such as El Camí IT6TR4 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, Pedals dels Ports, and Camino Natural del Matarraña - Algars, among others.

faqs.question: Is there a family-friendly section of the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the circular route directly around the reservoir is approximately 8.5 km and can be enjoyed by both cyclists and hikers. Additionally, the 4.53 km section from the dam to the Pena river is noted to be of low difficulty, making it a good option for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride.
